ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Dooley for and with Lawrence Thompson (Executor). Carolyn Farren for Hospice By The Bay. Tara Cooper appeared by telephone for American Red Cross.

The Court stated that it's a good resolution that there's been an agreement with regard to the approval of account and distribution. The accounting petition was taken under submission for review of the order. The Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel was taken off calendar without prejudice to adding it back on calendar.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of The Original Bank Statements
Off Calendar Mar-01-2011 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Dooley for and with Lawrence Thompson. Carolyn Farren for Hospice by The Bay. Tara Cooper appeared by telephone for American Red Cross.

Ms. Dooley stated that she has the accounting and that she will file it today. Status of filing of the original bank statements was taken off calendar. Status of filing of the account will be taken off calendar once the endorsed copy of the accounting is received. The Motion to Be Releived as Counsel will be continued to the same hearing date set on the accounting petition (April 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clauzel.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of The Original Bank Statements
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Jan-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Farren for Hospice by The Bay (Beneficiary). Nancy Dooley for and with Lawrence Thompson. Donna Collins (Accountant) also present.

Ms. Dooley stated that the accounts now have a balance of $532,661.00, that the accounting is being worked on and that she just saw the agreement between the Decedent and Mr. Thompson. In addition, Ms. Dooley stated that they provided reimbursement of $100,000.00 to the Estate last Friday in showing of good faith and that she will ask for approval of advanced distribution. Status of filing of the bank statements, status of filing of the accounting and the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel were continued to January 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order To Compel Executor To Account And To File For Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-23-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Meredith specially appearing for Nancy Dooley. Lisa Fialco for Hospice By The Bay. Lawrence Thompson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Thompson stated that he needed time to seek other legal representation, that he would try to work with Ms. Dooley but that he would proceed with seeking other Counsel. Mr. Meredith stated that he was not sure his Client was willing to work with Mr. Thompson due to the issues that have arisen, that Mr. Thompson needs a personal attorney and a probate attorney, that Mr. Thompson was not providing information and that there were communication issues. Ms. Fialco requested a Citation be issued so that documents/information can be provided regarding status of the assets of the Estate and asked if an account be provided immediately. The Court granted the Petition to Compel Executor to Account, etc. (subject to review of the writen order). Status of filing of the account was set for January 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Lawrence Thompson to file the most recent bank statements with the Court, and to provide copies to Hospice By The Bay and to all of the Beneficiaries. Status of filing of the original bank statements was set for December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Farren for Hospice By The Bay Foundation.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the file and noted that no one appeared this morning to oppose the petition. The Court inquired whether or not Ms. Farren had heard from the other beneficiaries. Ms. Farren stated that she had heard from the American Heart Association, the Red Cross, Friends of the Elderly, the American Cancer Society, and that none of them opposed the petition. The Court granted the petition (subject to a written order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli
